data.title: What's Going on with Lucid Stock Thursday
data.description:
Lucid Group shares fell about 6% Thursday after the company unveiled a new midsize electric-vehicle platform and named two initial models, Lucid Cosmos and Lucid Earth, both positioned under $50,000.

The company said the platform will use a new Atlas electric drive unit with identical front and rear housings to speed manufacturing and rely on smaller battery packs to cut costs while preserving range; Lucid also showed a two-seat robotaxi concept called Lunar and said it is in advanced talks with Uber Technologies, Inc. (UBER) on large-scale deployments.

Lucid reported full-year 2025 revenue of about $1.35 billion and delivered 15,841 vehicles, though losses remain large and margins deeply negative, leaving the firm some distance from profitability as it scales Gravity output and pursues software and services revenue.

Investors appeared to punish the stock on a mix of reasons: the investor-day details offered limited near-term margin relief, the company's heavy cash burn and wide losses remain salient, and several analysts cut targets or flagged production concerns, including RBC, Stifel and Cantor Fitzgerald, which likely amplified selling pressure.

data.title: Suspect in Michigan synagogue shooting was shot dead, law enforcement official says
data.description: A suspect involved in a shooting on Thursday at Temple Israel Synagogue in West Bloomfield, Michigan, was shot dead, a law enforcement official said.

data.title: Senegal's PM says contract for BP-operated GTA gas project is unfair
data.description:
Senegal's Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko said in a televised statement on Thursday that a gas contract for the Greater Tortue Ahmeyim (GTA) project operated by BP had been found to be one-sided and unfair after a review of strategic contracts.

Sonko's address on Thursday aimed to cover the outcome of a review of strategic contracts in a wide range of sectors launched by his government since coming to power in 2024.

He said the oil and gas sector was still under review and discussions underway with various partners, and the priority through the talks was to ensure the supply of gas.

He did not provide specific details about any discussions with BP.

Sonko promised to publish a document with the details of the contracts being studied. Senegal's review includes oil and gas contracts, fishing contracts and procurement contracts among others.

data.title: Global Indemnity Group posts underwriting improvement; accident-year combined ratio 92.2% in 2025
data.description:
Global Indemnity Group reported improved underwriting results in 2025, with the current accident-year combined ratio falling to 92.2% and an underwriting profit of $11 million in Q4. The company said operating income (excluding unrealized equity losses) was $40.2 million for the year and investment income was $62.7 million. Management highlighted continued short-duration, high-quality investments and ongoing digital transformation investments tied to its Katalyx distribution platform.

Financial Highlights

  • Q4 accident-year combined ratio: 89.3%; quarterly underwriting profit: $11 million.
  • Full-year current accident-year combined ratio: 92.2%; full-year reported accident-year combined ratio: 96.2% (including Q1 California wildfire impact).
  • Operating income (excluding after-tax unrealized losses on equity securities): $40.2 million for 2025 versus $42.9 million in 2024.
  • Investment income: $62.7 million in 2025 (average yield ~4.4%); net investment income for the quarter: $15.3 million.
  • Discretionary capital (consolidated equity in excess of rating-agency requirements): $284 million at year end.

Business Highlights

  • Underwriting trends improved sequentially after a large Q1 wildfire loss; excluding that event, quarterly accident-year combined ratios improved each quarter (94.8%, 94.7%, 93.2%, 92.2%).
  • Core Belmont gross written premiums (excluding terminated products) grew 9% to $401 million; notable segment growth: assumed reinsurance up 77% to $45 million, Vacant Express up 16%, Collectibles up 8%, Penn-America up 3%.
  • Retention at Penn-America remained strong at 70% despite heightened competition and reduced new business submissions in the fourth quarter.
  • Ongoing three-year digital transformation: year-two investments in software, infrastructure and data; 98% of data center servers moved to cloud and internal data migrated to a cloud-based Fabric Lakehouse to support future AI initiatives.
  • Restructuring and build-out costs remain elevated due to investments in Katalyx and talent; management expects expense ratio to remain level in 2026 with improvement beginning in 2027.

Original SEC Filing: Global Indemnity Group, LLC [ GBLI ] - 8-K - Mar. 12, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Wealthfront Stock Drops After It Posted Mixed Earnings Results — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Andrew Welsch

Shares of Wealthfront plummeted as much as 13% Thursday morning amid a broad market selloff and one day after the wealth management company reported mixed earnings results showing that hundreds of millions of dollars flowed out of customer accounts. The stock later pared its losses and was down about 5.6% as of 1:22 p.m.

Wealthfront swung to a loss of $135 million for its fiscal fourth quarter ended Jan. 31 because of one-time IPO-related expenses. That compares with a profit of $32 million for the same period a year ago. Revenue for the quarter came in at $96 million, a 16% increase from the same period a year ago. Analysts had expected revenue of $92.5 million, according to estimates compiled by FactSet.

Stocks were generally lower on Thursday because of mounting concerns about the war with Iran, oil prices, stubborn inflation, and trouble in private credit. The S&P 500 and the Nasdaq were down 1.1% and 1.3%, respectively, Thursday afternoon. The S&P 500 Financials Sector was down 1.5% to 811.11, putting it on pace for its lowest close since May 1, 2025, when it closed at 810.47.

Oil prices have soared as a result of the war with Iran, and if they remain elevated, they could put upward pressure on inflation at the same time the labor market is weakening in the U.S. Together, those trends complicate the expectations of Federal Reserve rate cuts. Wealthfront's stock is sensitive to the direction of interest rates, and more than two-thirds of its revenue in the most recent quarter came from its high-yield cash management offering.

When rates were high, Wealthfront attracted billions of dollars to that offering. But rate cuts last year diminished its appeal. Wealthfront said it suffered net outflows of $360 million for the quarter ending Jan. 31. That compares with net inflows of $2.7 billion for the same period a year earlier.

"While we continue to appreciate the clear value-proposition of the Wealthfront platform, we acknowledge the cash segment's ongoing sensitivity to rates (as well as seasonality) that has pressured additional asset growth," J.P. Morgan Securities analyst Kenneth Worthington writes in a March 11 research note. He rates Wealthfront stock Overweight, and he lowered his December 2026 price target to $10 from $16.

"While we acknowledge the ongoing debate around cash segment sensitivities and execution risk ahead, we think the long-term drivers of the Wealthfront platform remain intact and we like the risk/reward setup of the Wealthfront stock at this juncture and at this price," Worthington writes.

Keefe, Bruyette & Woods analyst Ryan Tomasello downgraded Wealthfront share to Market Perform from Outperform, writing that although Wealthfront's cash deposits turned positive in mid-February, tax season will likely drive withdrawal headwinds through April. Wealth management clients typically withdraw funds to pay tax bills. "This pushes out the potential for a more sustainable inflection closer to midyear at a time when we believe the prospect of additional Fed rate cuts will renew concerns over additional cash outflows," Tomasello writes in a March 11 research note.

Overall platform assets, which include Wealthfront's investment advice offering, rose to $94.1 billion from $80.2 billion thanks in part to market appreciation. The company also added more customers. The number of funded clients increased to 1.4 million from 1.2 million.

Wealthfront says it is driving net cross account transfers, meaning customers are moving money from the cash savings offering to the investment advice offering. That should help it offset lower revenue when customer dollars leave Wealthfront's cash management offering, though only to an extent because Wealthfront earns lower fees on investment advice than on cash management. Goldman Sachs analyst James Yaro wrote in a January research note that a dollar-for-dollar shift of assets from cash to investment advisory would result in a net revenue decline for the company. Wealthfront also said it is expanding its home lending offering, which may give it a new source of revenue. The company offers home loans to clients in Colorado, Texas, and California, and plans to make it available in other states later this year.

CEO David Fortunato said on the company's earnings call on Wednesday that Wealthfront can use technology to give clients a better experience and lower rates than competitors. "We aim to provide our clients home mortgage rates at least 50 basis points better than the national average, " he said. "While we are in early days, we're proud to have delivered on this objective on average in the states in which we operate today."

Write to Andrew Welsch at andrew.welsch@barrons.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Cosmetic Lens Maker Shanghai Moody Considers Sale That Could Value Company at $1 Billion
data.description:
Interest in China's cosmetic contact lens market may be drawing new deal attention. Shanghai Moody Technology Co., a fast-growing colored contact lens brand, is weighing strategic options that could lead to a sale valuing the company at about $1 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

The company is working with an adviser and may begin sounding out potential buyers in the coming months, the people said, asking not to be identified discussing private information. Deliberations remain ongoing and no final decisions have been made, suggesting the potential process could still evolve depending on investor interest and market conditions.

Founded in 2019, Moody focuses on cosmetic lenses and related consumer eyewear products. The company raised more than 1 billion yuan ($145 million) in a Series C funding round in 2021 backed by 5Y Capital and KKR & Co. , according to a press release, with other investors including GGV Capital and Hillhouse Investment. A representative for Moody did not respond to a request seeking comment.

data.title: U.S. customs agency says tariff refunds system between 40% and 80% complete
data.description:
By Tom Hals

The U.S. government's four-part system for refunding $166 billion in illegal tariff payments with interest is between 40% and 80% complete, according to a court filing on Thursday from a top customs official.

Brandon Lord, an official with U.S. Customs and Border Protection, said the agency is developing a four-part system it calls CAPE that will accept refund claims, mass process them, review them and issue refunds, according to a court filing.

Lord's filing in the U.S. Court of International Trade was made to comply with an order issued last week by Judge Richard Eaton as part of his directive to refund tariff payments.

data.title: McEwen Files 8K - Operations And Financial Condition >MUX
data.description:
McEwen Inc. (MUX) filed a Form 8K - Operations and Financial Condition - with the U.S Securities and Exchange Commission on March 12, 2026.

On March 12, 2026, McEwen Inc. (the "Company") issued a press release summarizing its fourth quarter and year-end 2025 financial and operating results together with certain operation updates. A copy of that press release is furnished with this report as Exhibit 99.1.

The information furnished under this Item 2.02, including the referenced exhibit, shall not be deemed "filed" for purposes of Section 18 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, nor shall it be deemed incorporated by reference in any filing under the Securities Act of 1933, except as shall be expressly set forth by reference to such filing.

The full text of this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/314203/000110465926026975/tm268667d1_8k.htm

Any exhibits and associated documents for this SEC filing can be retrieved at: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/314203/000110465926026975/0001104659-26-026975-index.htm

Public companies must file a Form 8-K, or current report, with the SEC generally within four days of any event that could materially affect a company's financial position or the value of its shares.

data.title: Nvidia, Broadcom, Micron Fall as Oil Risk Sends Shockwaves Through Tech
data.description:
Tech and AI stocks fell Thursday as surging oil prices added pressure on markets amid the intensifying U.S.-Israel-Iran conflict, now approaching its second week.

Brent crude futures jumped around 9%, and U.S. crude surged nearly 9.6%. Energy Secretary Chris Wright noted the Navy is not yet prepared to escort tankers through the Strait of Hormuz but could start by month-end. Analysts said markets are struggling to gauge the full impact of the ongoing conflict.

Chipmakers and AI-related companies saw broad declines. Nvidia fell about 2%, Advanced Micro Devices slid nearly 3%, and Broadcom dropped roughly 2%. Lumentum tumbled about 6%, Coherent slipped nearly 3%, and Micron Technology lost around 5%.

Equipment makers Lam Research and Applied Materials declined 5% and 4%, respectively.

data.title: Vail Analysts Look for Further Details on Strategy at Upcoming Investor Day — Market Talk
data.description: Morgan Stanley analysts say they're hoping for more clarity on several of Vail Resorts' strategies at the company's upcoming investor day. Investors will be paying close attention to Vail's expectations for returns on the investments it's making to improve operations and the guest experience, the analysts say. They'll also be looking to hear expectations for unit growth from recent pricing initiatives, including Vail's new pass discount for Gen Z skiers, they say. Another key question will be how Vail plans to re-accelerate visitation by re-engaging lower-usage passholders and translate its record guest satisfaction into improved conversion and retention, they say. The event, scheduled for March 17, will be the company's first investor day since Rob Katz returned as CEO last year, they say.(kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

data.title: JLL unveils Accelerate 2030 strategy, raises buyback authorization to $3B and sets long-term financial targets
data.description:
JLL introduced its Accelerate 2030 strategy and authorized an additional $2.2 billion in share repurchases, bringing total repurchase capacity to $3.0 billion.

Key Highlights:

  • Announced Accelerate 2030 multi-year strategy to deepen client relationships and scale AI & data capabilities.
  • Set long-term targets: 8% revenue growth, 12% adjusted EBITDA growth, 16% adjusted EPS growth (avg. through the cycle).
  • Board authorized $2.2B additional repurchase capacity, supplementing ~$800M remaining under prior authorizations to total $3.0B.
  • Plans an imminent $200M accelerated share repurchase as part of expanded buyback program.
  • Presentation and briefing recording furnished as Exhibits 99.1 and 99.2 and posted on JLL's investor website.

Original SEC Filing: JONES LANG LASALLE INC [ JLL ] - 8-K - Mar. 12, 2026

data.title: Oil Jumps to $100 as Hormuz Crisis Deepens; Dow Drops — WSJ
data.description:
By Caitlin McCabe

Oil prices touched $100 a barrel and U.S. stocks skidded amid worries of a protracted disruption to oil markets.

Brent crude futures jumped after President Trump said stopping the Iranian regime from getting nuclear weapons was a higher priority than oil prices. Brent recently traded near $98.50 a barrel after eclipsing $100 earlier in the day.

Earlier Thursday, the International Energy Agency slashed its forecast for oil-supply growth this year, and said the war in the Middle East was fueling the biggest disruption of global energy markets ever.

The Dow industrials fell more than 500 points, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq composite also down more than 1%.

Hopes of quickly restoring the flow of oil through the Strait of Hormuz are dimming, with Iran's new leader vowing to keep the key oil route closed and seven ships struck over the past day. Energy Secretary Chris Wright said the U.S. Navy wasn't yet ready to escort tankers through the waterway, but that he expected this to happen by the end of March.

Also adding to concerns: A report from Iran's semi-official Fars News Agency that Iran-backed groups could shut a crossing that controls traffic for vessels accessing the Suez Canal via the Red Sea.

The benchmark 10-year Treasury yield remained above 4.2% and the U.S. dollar advanced.

This item is part of a Wall Street Journal live coverage event. The full stream can be found by searching P/WSJL (WSJ Live Coverage).

data.title: Dick's Says Foot Locker Inventory Clearing Work Is Done — Market Talk
data.description: Dick's Sporting Goods says that it is now finished with its work of clearing out excess inventory for its recently-acquired Foot Locker. The brand had a lot of inventory "that wasn't very productive," says Dick's Executive Chairman Ed Stack. Dick's used markdowns in stores to clear out product and now, "this inventory at Foot Locker is probably cleaner than it has ever been," Stack says. "And that should bode well for our margins and our sales going forward." Dick's has "fixed that run-on sentence that was the Foot Locker shoe wall," he says. "That work is done." (nicholas.miller@wsj.com)

data.title: Income Opportunity Realty Investors 2025 10-K — Net Income $3.99M, EPS $0.98
data.description:
Income Opportunity Realty Investors reported net income of $3.99 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, with earnings per share of $0.98, driven primarily by interest income from notes receivable secured by income-producing properties. Interest income declined in 2025 versus 2024 due to lower interest rates, which reduced net income by approximately $0.7 million, while other income was negligible. The company expects cash and anticipated collections from notes and related-party receivables to be sufficient to meet 2026 operating needs.

Financial Highlights

  • Other income: $0.0 million (no revenue reported under Other income in 2025)
  • Net operating loss: $(0.383) million (operating loss reported as Net operating loss for 2025)
  • Net income: $3.990 million (Net income for year ended December 31, 2025)
  • Earnings per share - basic and diluted: $0.98 (Net Income Per Share as reported for 2025)

Business Highlights

  • Business model: Focused on earning interest income from notes receivable secured by income-producing properties in the Southern U.S.
  • Related-party operations: Significant activity and advisory management provided by a related-party advisor (Pillar), which affects sourcing and operations.
  • Revenue trend impact: Interest income declined in 2025 versus 2024 because of lower interest rates, reducing net income by roughly $0.7 million.
  • Liquidity outlook: Cash on hand and expected collections from notes and related-party receivables are projected to be sufficient to meet 2026 operating needs.
  • Asset management & risk: Uses periodic collectability assessments and fair-value measures for real estate collateral; non-performing loans are evaluated for impairment.

Original SEC Filing: INCOME OPPORTUNITY REALTY INVESTORS INC /TX/ [ IOR ] - 10-K - Mar. 12, 2026

data.title: Stryker Shipping Stock Slips As Cyberattack-Spurred Outage Drags On
data.description:
Key points:
  • Stryker said it has no indication of ransomware or malware.
  • Stryker also added that its teams are working to understand the full impact of the disruption to its internal environment. 
  • The company also clarified earlier in the day that its products like the Mako system for hip, knee and shoulder procedures, as well as other products like Vocera and LIFEPAK35, are fully safe to use.

Shares of medical technology company Stryker Corp. (SYK) traded lower for the third straight day on Thursday following a hack that left its ordering and shipping systems offline.

Bloomberg reported on Thursday that the company’s ordering and shipping systems are still offline following a cyberattack, claimed by pro-Iranian digital activist group Handala, on Wednesday.

Shares of the company traded nearly 2% lower at the time of writing.

Stryker Responds

“Stryker is responding to a global network disruption to our Microsoft environment as a result of a cyber attack. We have no indication of ransomware or malware and believe the incident is contained,” Stryker said in a statement 10:43 a.m. ET.

The company also clarified earlier in the day that its products like the Mako system for hip, knee and shoulder procedures, as well as other products like Vocera and LIFEPAK35, are fully safe to use.

Stryker also added that its teams are working to understand the full impact of the disruption to its internal environment. On Wednesday, the company said that it has launched an internal investigation into the incident with the support of external advisors and cybersecurity experts.

According to Bloomberg, the company has not confirmed whether an Iranian group is behind the incident yet. The risk of disruption to supplies depends on how much stock is held by hospitals, by local Stryker sales representatives, and by wholesalers and distributors, a person familiar with the matter told Bloomberg, while adding inventory held by distributors was expected to be sufficient to weather a short-term interruption.

Stryker is a global leader in medical technologies, and provides products and services in MedSurg, Neurotechnology and Orthopaedics. Based out of Kalamazoo, Michigan, the company makes makes joint replacements, robotic surgical systems, hospital beds, stretchers, and specialized imaging equipment.

How Did Stocktwits Users React?

On Stocktwits, retail sentiment around SYK stock stayed within the ‘bullish’ territory over the past 24 hours, while message volume rose from ‘high’ to ‘extremely high’ levels.

SYK stock has declined 7% over the past 12 months.

data.title: Vail's Challenges Seen Continuing if Customers Remain Discouraged With Low Snow — Market Talk
data.description: Vail Resorts could face further demand pressures into next year if its existing customers remain discouraged with historically tough snow conditions in the Rockies, Morgan Stanley analysts say in a note. Vail's visitation weakness has been most pronounced among its passholders, down 14% season-to-date versus a 6% decline for non-passholders, which adds uncertainty around attrition and renewal heading into pass sales for next season, the analysts say. However, Vail is seeing early green shoots from recent initiatives like its one-month advance tickets, and it's experiencing record-high guest satisfaction scores, they say. "Weather-driven volatility makes underlying demand trends and company execution difficult to isolate," they say. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)
